extra.reasoning:
The property NOT(rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b) holds for all inputs.

rec_drug_a is set true ONLY by r6 (priority 50), whose antecedent is the full conjunction (renal_impairment AND immunosuppressed AND bradycardia AND asthma AND pregnancy). No other rule sets rec_drug_a, and the default is false. So rec_drug_a=true requires all five of those inputs true.

rec_drug_b is affected by exactly two rules: r3 (priority 50, IF renal_impairment THEN rec_drug_b:=true) and r5 (priority 90, IF the full five-way conjunction THEN rec_drug_b:=false); default false.

Consider any input where rec_drug_a=true. Then the five-way conjunction holds, so r5 fires (rec_drug_b:=false at priority 90) and r3 also fires (rec_drug_b:=true at priority 50). Per the priority conflict-resolution rule, the highest-priority matching rule wins per output, so r5 (90) beats r3 (50): rec_drug_b is forced to false. No other rule can set rec_drug_b true (r1, r2, r4 only touch rec_drug_c).

Therefore, in every state where rec_drug_a is true, rec_drug_b is necessarily false. The two outputs can never be jointly true, so mutual exclusion holds across the entire 2^8 input space.
